title: Star Wars Missions 11: Bounty Hunters vs. Battle Droids
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions11.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 11:
Bounty Hunters vs. Battle Droids'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[July]] [[1998]]''[[Star Wars Missions 11: Bounty Hunters vs. Battle Droids]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0590583395 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0–0.5 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 9: Revolt of the Battle Droids]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story, which depicts the Rebel Alliance still occupying their base on Yavin 4, thus placing it in the first six months of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] before the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 10: Showdown in Mos Eisley]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 12: The Vactooine Disaster]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 11: Bounty Hunters vs. Battle Droids''''' is the eleventh adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[July]] [[1998]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''You are a bounty hunter. You are engaged in a fight to the death against a lethal squadron of battle droids. The droids' leader, Wuntoo Forcee Forwun, will stop at nothing in his quest to rule the galaxy. You must stand in his way. But be careful — the droids are not your only enemies. Other bounty hunters want to get to Forwun before you do. The reward is high — and the risks are extraordinary.'' ''Good luck.'' ==Plot summary== The [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Decimator (Star Destroyer)|''Decimator'']] heads through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] on a course for [[Vactooine]]. The ship, now under the control of [[12-4C-41's Droid Army]], is under the command of [[12-4C-41]], who regrets the recent loss at [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Nevertheless, he resolves to build his foundries on Vactooine, and return, to exact vengeance on [[EV-9D9/Legends|EV-9D9]]. Meanwhile, at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]], the biker [[Twitch (biker)|Twitch]] delivers a broken [[K4 security droid]] to 9D9, who begins interrogating it. Meanwhile, on [[Dennogra/Legends|Dennogra]], [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88B]] hunts [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who was recently on the planet dealing with the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] treasure merchant [[Sprool]]. Entering the [[Zio Snaffkin Cantina]], 88 finds the place completely wrecked, with all the patrons killed. The only patron of the cantina still alive is the notorious bounty hunter and 88's rival [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Soon after, [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]] and [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]] arrive on Dennogra, but the bounty hunters are soon met by a message droid from Jabba the Hutt, ordering them to hunt down Wuntoo's droid army. The bounty hunters all scramble to their ships and begin dogfighting, but only one of them escapes to the Vactooine system. There, the hunter encounters the ''Decimator'', which is hiding behind the largest [[moon of Vactooine]]. Wuntoo, in the ''Decimator'', launches [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] to intercept, but they are quickly swept aside by the hunter. The ''Decimator'', searching for its lost bombers, captures the bounty hunter in a tractor beam, bringing it into the landing bay. A [[K4 security droid]] boards the ship, but the hunter quickly dispatches it, before launching the ship's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] across the hangar floor, gaining the element of surprise. Despite this, the hunter is captured, and thrown in one of the ''Decimator''{{'s}} cells. The hunter does not give up, however, and defeats his guards, before questioning [[K-2PQ]]. Learning that the droids are renegades, and that they were hunting 9D9 and not Jabba, the hunter destroys 2PQ, and escapes the ship, programming the [[Drone barge/Legends|drone barge]] to travel to Tatooine on their way out. Upon returning to Tatooine, the hunter is set upon by [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]] and [[4-LOM/Legends|4-LOM]], but defeats them. Jabba, impressed, pays the hunter their fee. On Vactooine, however, Wuntoo's forces have subjugated the mining colony. On [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] detects that something is amiss when Vactooine is late for a shipment, and requests permission to investigate. ==Continuity== [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]] is labeled a "[[Gand/Legends|Gann]]" in this book. ==Appearances== {{App |characters= *[[12-4C-41]] *[[EV-9D9/Legends|EV-9D9]] *[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba
